user/testbin/threadtest/threadtest.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * threadtest.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: June 4, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *                      Piera Gueli             160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 
00010 
00011 
00012 #include <unistd.h>    
00013 #include <sys/types.h>   
00014 #include <err.h>      
00015 #include <stdio.h>      
00016 #include <sys/wait.h>  
00017 #include <stdlib.h>     
00018 #include <string.h>
00019 
00020 
00021 
00022 int main()
00023 {
00024     pid_t childpid; /* variable to store the child's pid */
00025     int status;
00026    
00027     /* now create new process */
00028     childpid = fork();
00029    
00030     if (childpid >= 0) /* fork succeeded */
00031     {
00032         if (childpid == 0) /* fork() returns 0 to the child process */
00033         {
00034             printf("\nCHILD: I am the child process!\n");
00035             printf("CHILD: Here's my PID: %d\n", getpid());
00036             printf("CHILD: Goodbye!\n");
00037             exit(0);
00038             
00039         }
00040         else /* fork() returns new pid to the parent process */
00041         {
00042             printf("\nPARENT: I am the parent process!\n");     
00043             printf("PARENT: I will now wait for my child to exit.\n");
00044             waitpid(childpid,&status,0);
00045             printf("\nPARENT: Here's my PID: %d\n", getpid());
00046             printf("PARENT: The value of my copy of childpid is %d\n", childpid);    
00047             printf("PARENT: Goodbye!\n");             
00048             exit(0);  /* parent exits */       
00049         }
00050     }
00051     else /* fork failed */
00052     {
00053         printf("Fork failed!\n"); 
00054         exit(0); 
00055     }
00056 }
